message:
refactor(sqlite): defer FTS consistency repair to explicit maintenance paths instead of every open

The FrankenStorage::open() path previously ran ensure_fts_consistency_via_
frankensqlite() on every database open, which could trigger heavyweight FTS
table rebuilds (DROP + CREATE + INSERT) while holding the database lock. This
blocked concurrent readers and made ordinary opens unpredictably slow when the
FTS generation marker was stale or missing.

FrankenStorage changes:
- Remove the ensure_fts_consistency_via_frankensqlite() call chain from open()
  so canonical opens only run migrations and schema object repair — never
  derived-index maintenance
- Add apply_open_stage_busy_timeout() to set a 5-second busy timeout before
  migrations, reducing lock contention on concurrent opens
- Expose ensure_search_fallback_fts_consistency() as a pub(crate) method for
  explicit maintenance callers to invoke when appropriate

SearchClient changes:
- Switch sqlite_guard() from FrankenStorage::open() to the new
  open_franken_readonly_storage_with_timeout() to ensure search reads never
  mutate the database or trigger FTS rebuilds
- Remove the nested retry-on-retryable-error pattern from sqlite_guard() since
  the timeout-based open handles busy waits internally
- Add graceful error handling in sqlite_fts_uses_message_id_column() so a
  broken or missing FTS table returns an empty result set instead of
  propagating errors up to the search path
- Add graceful error handling in the FTS fallback query execution so query
  failures return empty results with a warning instead of hard errors

Test updates:
- Update the FTS rebuild-on-reopen test to verify that search opens do NOT
  mutate the FTS rebuild generation marker and do NOT eagerly repair derived
  FTS artifacts — the test now asserts read-only behavior and calls
  ensure_search_fallback_fts_consistency() explicitly for the repair path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
